notes: The reported EntryPointNotFoundException from IL trimming could not be reproduced. Reporter's exact config (.NET 6 + SkiaSharp 2.88.1) is untestable on .NET 10 SDK — SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor 2.88.x references IJSUnmarshalledObjectReference which was removed in newer .NET. Testing with SkiaSharp 3.119.2 on .NET 10 Blazor WASM (published with trimming) shows the issue is fixed — canvas renders correctly with StrokeWidth, no EntryPointNotFoundException. Fix was via IsTrimmable assembly attribute and LibraryImport (source-generated P/Invoke) on .NET 7+ (PR #2917).

reproductionSteps.description: Create Blazor WASM project targeting net10.0
reproductionSteps.command: mkdir -p /tmp/skiasharp/repro/2221-latest && cd /tmp/skiasharp/repro/2221-latest && dotnet new blazorwasm -n Repro --framework net10.0

reproductionSteps.description: Add SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor 3.119.2 with WasmBuildNative enabled, and create Home.razor with reporter's SKPaint code using StrokeWidth

filesCreated.description: Blazor page with SKCanvasView using reporter's SKPaint code (StrokeWidth=2.0f, StrokeCap=Round, IsStroke=true) to trigger the P/Invoke call to sk_paint_set_stroke_width
Project file with WasmBuildNative=true and SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor 3.119.2

reproductionSteps.description: Publish with Release config which enables IL trimming for Blazor WASM
Serve trimmed published output via static file server and verify in browser with Playwright — checking for EntryPointNotFoundException on sk_paint_set_stroke_width
reproductionSteps.command: python3 serve.py (static file server on localhost:5113 serving publish/wwwroot/)
reproductionSteps.output: Browser console: SUCCESS: rendered 1600x1200. No errors. Canvas rendered cornflower blue background with white stroked rectangle. No EntryPointNotFoundException observed. StrokeWidth P/Invoke call works correctly after IL trimming.

versionResults.notes: SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor 2.88.9 fails on .NET 10 with TypeLoadException: IJSUnmarshalledObjectReference removed in newer .NET. Cannot test the trimming issue — reporter's .NET 6 SDK unavailable.
Published with IL trimming on .NET 10 Blazor WASM. Canvas renders correctly, StrokeWidth works, no EntryPointNotFoundException. Fix via IsTrimmable + LibraryImport.

actionability.reason: The trimming issue is fixed in SkiaSharp 3.x via IsTrimmable assembly attribute and source-generated LibraryImport (PR #2917). Testing confirms 3.119.2 works correctly with IL trimming on .NET 10 Blazor WASM. Reporter's .NET 6 + SkiaSharp 2.88.1 configuration is EOL.

actions.description: Close as fixed in SkiaSharp 3.x — trimming support added via IsTrimmable and LibraryImport

proposedResponse.body:
Thanks for the detailed report and workaround!

We attempted to reproduce the `EntryPointNotFoundException` for `sk_paint_set_stroke_width` caused by IL trimming in Blazor WASM.

The reporter's exact configuration (SkiaSharp 2.88.1 + .NET 6) could not be tested on the current .NET 10 SDK due to API incompatibilities in the Blazor Views package. However, testing with the latest **SkiaSharp 3.119.2** on .NET 10 Blazor WASM with a published/trimmed build confirms the issue is **resolved**.

**Tested versions:**
| Version | Result |
|---------|--------|
| 2.88.9 | ⚠️ Untestable (JSInterop API incompatibility with .NET 10) |
| 3.119.2 | ✅ Fixed — canvas renders correctly with `StrokeWidth` after trimming |

**What changed:** SkiaSharp 3.x added `[AssemblyMetadata("IsTrimmable", "True")]` and switched to source-generated `LibraryImport` on .NET 7+ (PR #2917), making all P/Invoke methods inherently trim-safe.

**Recommendation:** Upgrade to SkiaSharp 3.x on .NET 8+. If staying on .NET 6 (now EOL), the `ILLink.Descriptors.xml` workaround described in this issue remains the correct approach.

workarounds: Upgrade to SkiaSharp 3.x on .NET 8+ which has built-in trimming support
Add ILLink.Descriptors.xml with <assembly fullname="SkiaSharp"/> to prevent IL trimmer from stripping P/Invoke methods (reporter's workaround, for legacy .NET 6)
